source.title: Visual experience and subsequent sleep induce sequential plastic changes in putative inhibitory and excitatory cortical neurons.
source.venue: Proc Natl Acad Sci U S A 110(8):3101-6

confidence_rationale: Direct in-vivo evidence for a two-stage gate: waking monocular deprivation depresses fast-spiking interneurons (the instruction), and only subsequent NREM sleep -- with increased phase-locked principal-neuron firing -- executes the ocular-dominance shift. Supports MECH-334's sleep-gated crystallization AND INV-074's ordering requirement (correct competitive instruction must be encoded before crystallization). Moderate-high confidence; the strongest evidential value is the temporal ordering, which is exactly what the REE sleep dependencies (MECH-120/165) need.
failure_signatures: The ocular-dominance shift was evident ONLY after post-deprivation sleep, not after waking deprivation alone -- if REE crystallizes the diversity distribution during the active phase without a subsequent consolidation/sleep step, it will not reproduce the biological crystallization and MECH-334 would be mis-implemented.

Corrupted waking dynamics (incorrect gate-1 instruction) prevent sleep from crystallizing the right representation -- so MECH-334 cannot rescue a diversity distribution that was not competitively established during the MECH-333 open window; this couples MECH-334's success to INV-074's necessity condition and makes them jointly falsifiable.

mapping.source_claim_statement: Monocular deprivation during waking depresses fast-spiking interneuron firing and shifts their responses (instructive gate 1); during subsequent NREM sleep, principal-neuron firing increases and becomes phase-locked to slow-wave and spindle oscillations, and the ocular-dominance shift -- proportional to the waking interneuron changes -- emerges only after this post-deprivation sleep (executive gate 2).
mapping.ree_translation: Aton et al. give MECH-334 its execution timing: crystallization of the consolidated representation is sleep-gated and requires a correct waking instruction first. This directly motivates REE's coupling of crystallization closure to the sleep mechanisms MECH-334 already depends on (MECH-120 SWY downscaling, MECH-165 replay diversity), and operationalises INV-074's ordering: competitive weight must be established before the closure/consolidation step can lock it in.
mapping.mapping_caveat: The two-stage gate is demonstrated for ocular-dominance plasticity in cat visual cortex; REE's analogue is an offline consolidation phase crystallizing a diversity distribution. The transfer is to the abstract sequence 'waking competitive instruction -> sleep-phase crystallization', not to FS-interneuron physiology or cortical oscillations specifically. Evidences MECH-334's execution timing and INV-074's ordering, not the molecular lock (Pizzorusso/Morishita).
mapping.source_context: Freely behaving cats; continuous extragranular recordings of fast-spiking interneurons and putative principal neurons across waking monocular deprivation and post-deprivation sleep.

confidence_components.notes: PNAS, Frank lab, careful continuous in-vivo recordings. Tagged to both MECH-334 (sleep-gated execution) and INV-074 (instruction-before-crystallization ordering) because it genuinely evidences both and makes them jointly falsifiable. Held at 0.82: high-value for temporal ordering, with the usual cat-visual-cortex -> REE-consolidation abstraction discount.
